weather_report:
Subiaco dozes under a soft patter, streets lacquered with rain and the night air smelling faintly of eucalyptus and bitumen. Clouds are thick and close, so there is no hint of sun; the UV is essentially asleep too. Clarity is gentle rather than sharp, with drizzle smudging the edges of streetlights and headlights. It feels cool and a little damp, the sort of hour when the house is quiet and the kettle sounds louder than usual.

Toward the small hours and into dawn, more light rain drifts through, keeping the morning subdued. Sunshine will be stingy early, and the UV stays low until well after sunrise; if the cloud loosens later, it may rouse a little, but don’t count on any blaze. Dress for a walk between showers: a light waterproof, closed shoes, and an umbrella you won’t mind carrying. It’s pleasant weather for unhurried tasks—replying to the message you put off, folding laundry, or, once the rain eases, a short stroll to admire rain-polished jacarandas and pick up bread while the world is still hushed.
